channel.title: Projects on Youri Schuurmans
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Youri Schuurmans

item.title: Alfam
item.description:
Within Alfam I work within the team responsible for the client experience, where we focus mainly on the already-onboarded customers. We created and maintain the main portal used by the customers, called MijnDefam. In this portal you are able to manage your loans, change your details, and download several reports.

My role in this project:

Consultant/Developer

My Responsibilities:

Implementation and maintaining of the MijnDEFAM portal

  • Integration of IdentityServer within the portal.
  • Monitoring status and uptime of the services supporting the portal.
  • Implementing new requirements based on changes in the law.
  • Implementing changes for assisting customer service.

item.title: Boomiu
item.description: Boomiu merges the pressure created in stressful co-op games like Space Team with the fun to be had in party games like Mario Party. The goal of Boomiu is to play as many minigames as possible before the time runs out and the bomb explodes. After each minigame you pass the phone to the next player and play the next minigame with that player, then try to survive as long as possible!

item.title: Bronverbinding
item.description: Group meetings organized by Angélique Bijlaart and Henk Raaimakers. Both of these are well known for energetic and consciousness work. The objective of the meetings is to become more balanced, to tap into your inner strength, peace, and freedom to experience yourself. Your personal blocks are cleared on a physical, emotional, mental and spiritual level. Together with a group, it becomes possible to join forces and create a powerful energetic field, in which your personal fields can be addressed.

item.title: Clone runner
item.description:
Clonerunner was my first project I aimed to finish from start to end, since this was the first time I got the opportunity to develop something from scratch, without a set goal. This gave me the motivation to work long days and even longer nights to create something I would like to play.

However, the longer I worked on the project, the harder it became to add new content. I started to realise the mistakes I had made, the wrong decisions that would prevent me from adding certain features unless I rewrote that piece of code. After many attempts I decided to drop the project, promising myself to try to use what I learned as a stepping stone for better projects.

item.title: DIM @ BlewScreen
item.description:
DIM is the name of the project and contains two games under the same umbrella: DIM Energizer and DIM Move Your Brain.

Move Your Brain is a math game where kids in a classroom have to perform a certain gesture together to reveal a math question, and then answer this math question by performing another gesture that matches the gesture with the correct answer on the board. This way the class is constantly in motion, from the moment the game starts until the moment the game is over.

item.title: Myo God Game
item.description:
The Myo bracelet is a device you can use to control your computer and other devices using motion and finger gestures. It records muscle activity through sensors across the arm to detect when certain muscles are under tension. Using this information the bracelet can determine how your fingers are positioned relative to your arm or figure out which finger gesture you are making.

We used the Myo bracelet as the main controller in a game jam game we made as a group. The game jam was hosted by our school and the given theme was “New Tech”. The school gathered all of the available tools and technologies for the students to choose from, so you could choose one or more technologies to use in your project.

item.title: Portfolio Website
item.description: A whole website is written from scratch using ASP.NET MVC, a C# web framework which enables C# developers to create websites using the MVC code pattern. The most important thing about a portfolio website to me is the ability to edit the content on the portfolio without having to dig into the code and edit the pages, so I built a custom Content Management System (CMS) to edit all of the pages you see on this website.

item.title: Predicting Potential Drug Lab Locations
item.description:
The Netherlands, and in particular Noord-Brabant has a huge drugs problem. They are supposedly the world-leader in synthetic drugs globally. This motivated the province to think about this problem, and about possible solutions. One of the options that has not been explored previously is data-science. The police, the city’s and the province itself has heaps of data lying around.

The Province contacted us to take on this data science challenge and create a model to predict the locations of future drug labs using the heaps of data collected over the years by the government.

item.title: Procedural World Generation
item.description:
I wanted to learn how to generate and apply noise maps, specifically for use in games, because I always loved the possibility of exploring an infinite world, and one that could be generated consistently across different players without having to send over an entire world.

Using this as my main starting point I tried to create a deterministic world generator that would generate the same world every time, for every player, down to the smallest details such as rotations and placements of objects. I wanted to be able to apply one of these techniques in a later project to keep the amount of data that has to be transferred as low as possible without having to compromise in gameplay.
